intro: Visualizing sales pipeline data turns a wall of CRM rows into something your whole team can actually act on. A good pipeline chart shows you instantly where deals are stacking up, which stages are leaking, and how much revenue is at risk. If your data is in a CSV, you can build these charts in Excel or Google Sheets in a few minutes, or skip the manual steps and upload directly to Distill.

whatIs.heading: What Is a Sales Pipeline Chart?
whatIs.body: A sales pipeline chart maps your open deals across each stage of the sales process, from first contact through to closed. It works best with data that has at least a stage column and a deal value column. The most common formats are funnel charts (showing volume shrinkage by stage) and bar charts grouped by stage or rep.

whenToUse.heading: When Should You Use a Sales Pipeline Chart?
bullets: You want to spot which stage is causing the most deal drop-off so you know where to focus coaching or process fixes.
You need a weekly snapshot of forecasted revenue by stage to share with leadership without building a custom report.
You are comparing pipeline health across territories, reps, or product lines to find where one team is outperforming another.

excelSteps.heading: How to Make a Sales Pipeline Chart in Excel
steps: Step 1. Export your CRM data as a CSV and open it in Excel. Make sure you have at least two columns: one for deal stage and one for deal value.
Step 2. Create a summary table using a pivot table. Go to Insert > PivotTable, select your data range, and place 'Stage' in Rows and 'Deal Value' in Values (set to Sum).
Step 3. Sort the pivot table so stages appear in order from earliest to latest in your sales process.
Step 4. Select the pivot table and go to Insert > Chart. Choose a Bar chart (clustered) or a Funnel chart if you are on Excel 2019 or later.
Step 5. Right-click the chart and choose 'Select Data' to adjust series labels and confirm each stage name reads correctly.
Step 6. Add a chart title like 'Open Pipeline by Stage', format axis labels for currency, and delete gridlines for cleaner presentation.

sheetsSteps.heading: How to Make a Sales Pipeline Chart in Google Sheets
steps: Step 1. Import your CRM CSV into Google Sheets via File > Import, selecting 'Replace spreadsheet' and comma as the delimiter.
Step 2. Insert a pivot table: click Insert > Pivot table, use the current sheet as the data range, and open the pivot editor on a new sheet.
Step 3. Add 'Stage' to Rows and 'Deal Value' to Values, set the aggregation to SUM, and sort rows by the natural order of your pipeline stages.
Step 4. Select the pivot table summary, then click Insert > Chart. In the Chart Editor, choose 'Bar chart' under chart type.
Step 5. Under the 'Customize' tab, set the chart and axis titles. Format the vertical axis as currency using the number format field.
Step 6. Click the three-dot menu on the chart and select 'Publish chart' to generate a shareable link, or copy it into a Slides deck for your next team meeting.

distillSection.heading: A Faster Way: Skip the Manual Steps with Distill
distillSection.body: If your pipeline data is already in a CSV, Distill removes the pivot table setup entirely. Upload the file and Distill reads your columns, picks the right chart types automatically, and generates a full visual dashboard in seconds. You get a funnel view, a bar breakdown by stage, and an AI-written narrative that calls out your biggest drop-off points, all without touching a formula. The dashboard is shareable with a single link, so you can send it to your team or drop it in a Slack message without any export steps. Distill's free tier handles CSV files up to 1,000 rows with no account required.

gifAlt: A screen recording of a sales pipeline CSV being dragged into Distill, which instantly generates a funnel chart and bar chart broken down by deal stage.

faqs.q: Can I visualize sales pipeline data without Excel?
faqs.a: Yes. Google Sheets handles pipeline charts well and is free to use. If you want something faster with no setup, Distill lets you upload a CSV and get charts instantly without any spreadsheet work.

faqs.q: What columns do I need in my CSV to build a pipeline chart?
faqs.a: At minimum you need a column for deal stage and a column for deal value. Columns for close date, rep name, or product line let you slice the data further, but the core chart works with just those two.
Yes. Distill's free tier supports CSV files up to 1,000 rows and includes all chart types. No account is required to try it.
